title: BalCapRL: A Balanced Framework for RL-Based MLLM Image Captioning
gist: Apple researchers propose BalCapRL, an RL framework that jointly optimizes utility, reference coverage, and linguistic quality for MLLM image captioning, using GDPO-style reward normalization and length-conditional reward masking to beat vanilla GRPO across LLaVA and Qwen models.

insights.claim: Existing captioning-RL methods emphasize a narrow notion of caption quality, inducing trade-offs: utility-oriented objectives can encourage noisy, hallucinated, or overlong captions, while arena-style objectives favor fluent but generic, less useful descriptions.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Names the hallucination/verbosity failure mode of single-objective RL — a recurring concern for any product relying on multimodal model outputs.
insights.quote: utility-oriented objectives can encourage noisy, hallucinated, or overlong captions that improve downstream question answering while harming fluency

insights.claim: BalCapRL is a balanced RL framework that jointly optimizes utility-aware correctness, reference coverage, and linguistic quality for image captioning.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Multi-objective reward design is becoming the standard fix for RL-induced degradation — watch this pattern spread across alignment work.
insights.quote: we propose a more balanced RL framework that jointly optimizes utility-aware correctness, reference coverage, and linguistic quality

insights.claim: Applying GDPO-style reward-decoupled normalization to continuous-valued captioning rewards improves performance over vanilla GRPO, and length-conditional reward masking yields a more suitable length penalty.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Specific technique (reward-decoupled normalization) shown to beat GRPO — a concrete recipe for teams doing RL on multimodal models.
insights.quote: we apply GDPO-style reward-decoupled normalization to continuous-valued captioning rewards and show that it improves performance over vanilla GRPO

insights.claim: Across LLaVA-1.5-7B and Qwen2.5-VL 3B and 7B base models, BalCapRL delivers peak gains of +13.6 DCScore, +9.0 CaptionQA, and +29.0 CapArena.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Quantified multi-metric gains on widely used open base models make the recipe directly evaluable by practitioners.
insights.quote: with peak gains of +13.6 DCScore, +9.0 CaptionQA, and +29.0 CapArena across different models
